heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Howard Hawks. Written by Hager Wilde and Dudley Nichols.
clean: Directed by Howard Hawks. Written by Hager Wilde and Dudley Nichols.

heading: Susan Vance
raw: [limping after losing a heel] I was born on the side of a hill!
clean: I was born on the side of a hill!

heading: Susan Vance
raw: [reading a letter about Baby] "He's three years old, gentle as a kitten, and likes dogs." I wonder whether Mark means that he eats dogs or is fond of them?
clean: "He's three years old, gentle as a kitten, and likes dogs." I wonder whether Mark means that he eats dogs or is fond of them?

heading: Dr. David Huxley
raw: Now it isn't that I don't like you, Susan, because, after all, in moments of quiet, I'm strangely drawn toward you, but, well, there haven't been any quiet moments.
clean: Now it isn't that I don't like you, Susan, because, after all, in moments of quiet, I'm strangely drawn toward you, but, well, there haven't been any quiet moments.

heading: Dr. David Huxley
raw: When a man is wrestling a leopard in the middle of a pond, he's in no position to run!
clean: When a man is wrestling a leopard in the middle of a pond, he's in no position to run!

heading: Dr. Fritz Lehman
raw: That brings out a sort of pear-shaped tone, you see?
clean: That brings out a sort of pear-shaped tone, you see?

heading: Dialogue
raw: David: Susan, you've got to get out of this apartment!
clean: Susan, you've got to get out of this apartment!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan: I can't, I have a lease.
clean: I can't, I have a lease.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan: [in tears] You mean you want me to go home?
clean: You mean you want me to go home?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan: You mean you don't want me to help you any more?
clean: You mean you don't want me to help you any more?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan: After all the fun we've had?
clean: After all the fun we've had?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan: And after all the things I've done for you?
clean: And after all the things I've done for you?

heading: Dialogue
raw: David: That's what I mean.
clean: That's what I mean.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan: Well, don't you worry, David, because if there's anything that I can do to help you, just let me know and I'll do it.
clean: Well, don't you worry, David, because if there's anything that I can do to help you, just let me know and I'll do it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: David: [nervously] Well, er... don't do it until I let you know.
clean: Well, er... don't do it until I let you know.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Susan: Anyway, David, when they find out who we are they'll let us out.
clean: Anyway, David, when they find out who we are they'll let us out.

heading: Dialogue
raw: David: When they find out who you are they'll pad the cell.
clean: When they find out who you are they'll pad the cell.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Elisabeth: Well, you look perfectly idiotic in those clothes.
clean: Well, you look perfectly idiotic in those clothes.

heading: Dialogue
raw: David: These aren't my clothes!
clean: These aren't my clothes!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Elisabeth: Well, where are your clothes?
clean: Well, where are your clothes?

heading: Dialogue
raw: David: I've lost my clothes!
clean: I've lost my clothes!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Elisabeth: But why are you wearing these clothes?
clean: But why are you wearing these clothes?

heading: Dialogue
raw: David: Because I just went gay all of a sudden!
clean: Because I just went gay all of a sudden!
